name: Vietnam & Cambodia: From Ha Long Bay to Angkor Wat
meta_desc: 16 days across Vietnam and Cambodia, from Hanoi and Ha Long Bay to the caves of Phong Nha, imperial Hue, Hoi An and the temples of Angkor Wat.
tagline: 16 days across two extraordinary countries — from the karsts of Ha Long Bay and the caves of Phong Nha to sunrise over Angkor Wat.
hero_alt: Angkor Wat temple complex at sunrise in Siem Reap
overview: Experience the very best of Southeast Asia on this unforgettable 16-day journey through Vietnam and Cambodia. From the bustling streets of Hanoi and the emerald waters of Ha Long Bay to the tranquil rice terraces of Pu Luong, the spectacular caves of Phong Nha, the imperial history of Hue, the lantern-lit charm of Hoi An, and the magnificent temples of Angkor Wat, this itinerary showcases the incredible diversity of two remarkable countries. Designed for travellers seeking the perfect balance of culture, nature, history and authentic local experiences, this journey combines iconic highlights with hidden gems rarely found on standard tours. Whether it's your first visit to Southeast Asia or you're looking for a comprehensive journey through two extraordinary destinations, this itinerary offers memories that will last a lifetime.
highlights: Explore Hanoi aboard a vintage Army Jeep or motorbike
Discover the secrets of Vietnam's famous coffee culture
Cruise through the spectacular limestone islands of Ha Long Bay overnight
Trek through the breathtaking rice terraces of Pu Luong Nature Reserve
Visit local Thai ethnic minority communities
Explore the magnificent caves of Phong Nha-Ke Bang National Park
Discover Vietnam's wartime history at the Vinh Moc Tunnels and DMZ
Explore the Imperial City of Hue
Drive across the spectacular Hai Van Pass
Join a hands-on Vietnamese cooking class in Hoi An
Experience a traditional bamboo basket boat ride
Visit Cambodia's inspiring APOPO HeroRATs Centre
Watch sunrise over the magnificent Angkor Wat
Explore the incredible temples of the Angkor Archaeological Park

itinerary.title: Welcome to Hanoi
itinerary.desc: Arrive in Vietnam's vibrant capital and settle into your hotel in the Old Quarter. Meet your guide and enjoy a welcome dinner while experiencing your first taste of authentic Vietnamese cuisine.

itinerary.title: Discover Hanoi
itinerary.desc: Explore Hanoi aboard a vintage Army Jeep or Motorbike, visiting famous landmarks including Train Street, Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum, West Lake, and the Old Quarter before learning the art of Vietnamese coffee during an interactive coffee workshop.

itinerary.title: Ha Long Bay Cruise
itinerary.desc: Travel to the spectacular UNESCO World Heritage Site of Ha Long Bay and board your overnight luxury cruise. Explore hidden caves, kayak through limestone islands, enjoy swimming opportunities, and watch the sunset over one of the world's most beautiful seascapes.

itinerary.title: Ha Long Bay to Pu Luong
itinerary.desc: Enjoy your final morning cruising through Ha Long Bay before travelling inland to the peaceful mountains of Pu Luong Nature Reserve, home to spectacular rice terraces and traditional Thai villages.

itinerary.title: Trek Through Pu Luong
itinerary.desc: Spend the day trekking through lush valleys, picturesque rice terraces and remote villages while learning about the traditions and daily life of the local Thai ethnic minority communities.

itinerary.title: Journey to Phong Nha
itinerary.desc: Travel south to the peaceful village of Phong Nha, surrounded by dramatic limestone mountains and the gateway to one of Asia's most impressive cave systems.

itinerary.title: Explore Phong Nha
itinerary.desc: Visit the famous Phong Nha Cave, cruise along the Son River, enjoy local cuisine, and experience the relaxed countryside atmosphere of this beautiful UNESCO National Park.

itinerary.title: Paradise Cave & Commander Cave
itinerary.desc: Discover two of Vietnam's most remarkable cave systems while learning about their incredible geological formations and important role during the Vietnam War.

itinerary.title: Vietnam's Wartime History
itinerary.desc: Travel towards Hue via the Vinh Moc Tunnels and the former Demilitarized Zone, gaining a fascinating insight into Vietnam's modern history.

itinerary.title: Imperial Hue
itinerary.desc: Explore the magnificent Imperial City, ancient citadel and rich heritage of Vietnam's last royal dynasty before enjoying free time to discover Hue's famous cuisine and vibrant markets.

itinerary.title: Scenic Journey to Hoi An
itinerary.desc: Drive across the breathtaking Hai Van Pass, visit the towering Lady Buddha overlooking Da Nang, and arrive in the enchanting UNESCO-listed Ancient Town of Hoi An.

itinerary.title: Hoi An Cooking Experience
itinerary.desc: Visit Hoi An's colourful local market before enjoying a hands-on Vietnamese cooking class and a traditional bamboo basket boat ride through the peaceful coconut forests.

itinerary.title: Vietnam to Cambodia
itinerary.desc: Transfer to Da Nang Airport for your short flight to Siem Reap, Cambodia, where you'll begin the second chapter of your Southeast Asian adventure.

itinerary.title: Discover Siem Reap
itinerary.desc: Visit the inspiring APOPO HeroRATs Centre to learn about Cambodia's remarkable landmine clearance programme before exploring the fascinating Lotus Silk Farm and discovering traditional Khmer craftsmanship.

itinerary.title: Sunrise at Angkor Wat
itinerary.desc: Wake before dawn to witness one of the world's most unforgettable sunrises over Angkor Wat before spending the day exploring the magnificent temples of the Angkor Archaeological Park, including Bayon, Ta Prohm and Angkor Thom City.

itinerary.title: Farewell Cambodia
itinerary.desc: Enjoy breakfast before transferring to Siem Reap International Airport for your onward journey home.

accommodation.hotel: La Siesta Classic Ma May Hotel (or similar)
La Siesta Hoi An Resort (or similar)

inclusions: Airport arrival and departure transfers throughout Vietnam and Cambodia
One-night Ha Long Bay luxury cruise
Entrance fees and sightseeing as listed
exclusions: International and regional flights (we recommend preferred flight numbers and departure times for seamless connections)
travel_notes: Best travel season: October to April
Comfortable walking shoes are recommended for sightseeing and light trekking
Modest clothing covering shoulders and knees is required when visiting temples, including Angkor Wat and the Imperial City of Hue
Vietnam uses the Vietnamese Dong (VND), while Cambodia accepts both the Cambodian Riel (KHR) and US Dollar (USD)
This itinerary includes a combination of cultural sightseeing, scenic walks and light trekking, suitable for most travellers with a moderate level of fitness
flexible_note: This itinerary has been carefully designed to showcase the very best of Vietnam and Cambodia but can be fully customised to suit your interests, travel style, accommodation preferences, budget and available travel time. Whether you'd like to extend your stay, upgrade your accommodation, add additional destinations or enjoy more leisure time, our team will create a personalised journey that's perfect for you.
